title: dortmanna quadrangularis
intro: Dortmanna quadrangularis, also known as the Black Cardamom, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Asia and the Himalayas, characterized by its dark green, quadrangular leaves and purple flowers. The medicinal properties of the Dortmanna quadrangularis plant are attributed to its essential oils, including limonene and eugenol, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Dortmanna quadrangularis include its ability to alleviate nausea, relieve pain, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and seeds of the Dortmanna quadrangularis plant are commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
